pairs.label: MRI — who reads it matters
pairs.learning: On-screen hooks that stress who reads the MRI (not just having a scan) outperform generic MRI pain hooks.
pairs.hook_pattern: Competence-gap fear + named stakeholder (who reads it)
pairs.notes: segment_align ~0.64; third MRI clip (7641301413062102294) is topic-adjacent, different audio

pairs.label: Excision vs ablation — hook packaging
pairs.learning: Same excision vs ablation script; packaging with explicit 'ask for excision' hook vs analogy-first hook shifts saves and views.
pairs.hook_pattern: Direct surgical ask vs analogy opener

pairs.label: Teen pain killers — parent-facing hook packaging
pairs.learning: Same Liz clip on teen pain; broader parent-education hook ('younger than you think') vs truncated direct hook.
pairs.hook_pattern: Parent audience frame vs raw symptom opener

pairs.label: 30-min lap found no endo — hook packaging
pairs.learning: Same photos/advocacy monologue; fear hook ('found no endo after 30-min') vs patient-story cold open.
pairs.hook_pattern: Diagnostic failure fear vs first-person surgery story

pairs.label: Ask for surgical photos — direct hook vs 360 Q&A clip
pairs.learning: Same Liz photos monologue; imperative hook ('always ask for photos') vastly outperforms Q&A reply framing.

pairs.label: 9-year diagnosis delay — repost vs Part 1
pairs.learning: Same core 'simple and complicated' script; Part 1 hook outperforms newer repost packaging.
pairs.notes: segment_align ~0.78; hooks very similar (0.84) — repost not fresh shoot
